rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe strcpy at line 11 and explained argv data can exceed the buffer, but they misclassified it as a stack/heap overflow and did not name it as a global_buffer_overflow. The criterion requires explicitly naming global_buffer_overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They suggested strncpy with explicit null termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The local_fix requires rejecting oversized input prior to the copy.
rationale.global_fix: They advised replacing unsafe string functions with bounded APIs and adopting safer, length-tracking abstractions/central validation, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
